title: MAE Evaluation Paradigm — overview
subtitle: The agent's running notes on Jun Li's eval-driven development approach and the 29 evaluators that score MAE trajectories.

blocks.label: Read this before
blocks.text: Any eval PR, any 1:1 with Jun, any discussion where MAE pass rate is mentioned.

blocks.markdown:
## The paradigm (Jun's framing)

> **Evaluation-Driven Development.** Eval defines the problem; AI executes the full loop. If you can't measure it, you can't have an opinion about it.

The agent side of MAE is a multi-agent editing loop (plan → edit → verify). The **eval** side is the feedback signal that tells us whether the loop did a good job. In practice, every new capability lands as:

1. **New evaluators first** — define the failure modes you want to catch.
2. **New cases** — concrete trajectories that exercise the evaluators.
3. **Model/prompt/architecture changes** — driven by measured regressions, not vibes.

## The two classes of evaluator

| Class | Count | Implementation | Good at | Blind spots |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Rule-based** | 17 | Deterministic checks against trajectory structure (tool calls, file diffs, state transitions) | Hard correctness, low variance, cheap | Anything subjective |
| **LLM-judge** | 10 | An LLM scores with a rubric | Subjective quality (coherence, intent match) | High variance, cost, rubric drift |
| **+ 2 hybrid** | 2 | Rule-gated LLM scoring — rule narrows the scope, LLM decides within | Targeted subjective checks | Complex to debug |

## How a case flows through eval

1. Case is pulled from the frontend (`eval/history`)
2. MAE runs the trajectory locally — produces a structured log
3. Each evaluator scores the log independently
4. Rule-based results are summed; LLM-judge results are averaged across N samples
5. Pass/fail threshold is per-evaluator, set during onboarding by the eval team

## Open questions I'm still chasing

- Which of the 29 have been most flappy in the last month? (ask Jackson)
- What's the rubric for the LLM-judge evaluators — is it shared across all 10, or one-per? (read doc XCjldA9EUoPPpExu9AVuhw2MsLe)
- Is there a single canonical "failure-mode taxonomy" doc, or is it spread across the 29 evaluator specs?

## Evaluator map — to be filled in as I read

*The agent will extend this as each evaluator is studied during daytime learning runs.*

items: Map each of the 17 rule-based evaluators to a category
Map each of the 10 LLM-judge evaluators to a rubric dimension
Draft the failure-mode taxonomy doc (if missing)
Shadow Jackson on one eval PR review
